source.title: Prediction error and regularity detection underlie two dissociable mechanisms for computing the sense of agency

confidence_rationale: Wen & Haggard demonstrate that sense of agency has two components: prediction-error-based (comparator model) and regularity-based. The comparator mechanism directly parallels REE's SD-003 self-attribution via prediction error comparison. Supports MECH-025's responsibility-linked control aspect. Confidence moderated by indirect mapping -- the paper studies subjective agency experience, not a computational control mode.
failure_signatures: The paper studies subjective sense of agency in humans, not computational control regimes -- the link to 'action mode' as an architectural feature requires a mapping leap.
Both agency mechanisms (prediction error + regularity) operate continuously, not as a discrete mode switch.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sense of agency is computed by two dissociable mechanisms: (1) a comparator that matches predicted and actual sensory outcomes (prediction error), and (2) a regularity detector that identifies statistical regularities between actions and outcomes. These operate independently and combine to determine whether the agent perceives itself as the cause of an event.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-025's responsibility-linked control requires the agent to attribute causal agency to itself. The comparator mechanism described here is the biological analogue of REE's SD-003: E2(z_t, a_actual) - E2(z_t, a_cf) computes the agent's causal signature by comparing actual vs counterfactual predictions. When this comparison yields high self-attribution, the agent enters action-doing mode with elevated precision.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper measures subjective sense of agency via behavioral tasks (intentional binding, agency ratings). REE implements self-attribution computationally via E2 counterfactual comparison. The mapping assumes that the computational mechanism underlying subjective agency experience is functionally analogous to REE's E2 counterfactual -- a reasonable but unproven assumption.
mapping.source_context: Human behavioral experiments on sense of agency, intentional binding paradigm, UCL cognitive neuroscience

confidence_components.notes: Well-designed human behavioral study in a respected journal. Mapping fidelity moderate because the paper addresses subjective agency rather than computational control architecture. Transfer risk moderate: the comparator mechanism concept transfers well but the specific experimental operationalization (intentional binding) is far from REE's implementation.
